column7: 3*Band 8 Version*: One effective way to combat doping in professional sports is to impose far stricter penalties on offenders. If sporting organizations introduced heavier financial sanctions and lengthy suspensions for athletes who use prohibited substances, many competitors would be far less likely to risk damaging their careers and reputations. In addition, anti-doping authorities should invest in more advanced testing technology and screening procedures. Doing so would help eliminate loopholes within the current system and significantly improve the accuracy of test results. For example, after the UFC invested heavily in state-of-the-art detection equipment, a considerable number of fighters were found to have violated anti-doping regulations.
column8: 3*Band 9 Version*: A highly effective measure to deter the use of performance-enhancing substances is the enforcement of severe disciplinary and financial penalties against offenders. Were sporting authorities to impose lengthy bans, revoke titles, and levy substantial fines on athletes found guilty of doping, many competitors would likely reconsider engaging in such unethical practices due to the potentially irreversible damage to both their careers and public reputations. Equally important is the need to strengthen anti-doping infrastructure through investment in cutting-edge detection technology and more rigorous testing protocols. By closing existing regulatory loopholes and enhancing the reliability of screening procedures, sports organizations would be better equipped to identify violations accurately and consistently. This can already be seen in the UFC, where significant investment in advanced testing systems resulted in numerous fighters testing positive for banned substances.
